question.text: Under Section 6(3) of SCRA, who has the power to call upon a recognized stock exchange or any member thereof to furnish information relating to the affairs of the stock exchange?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 6(3) clearly states that SEBI, in the interest of trade or the public interest, may by order in writing call upon a recognised stock exchange or any member to furnish information. **The Trap:** The Central Government is tempting because SCRA originally vested powers with the Central Government, but post-amendment, SEBI exercises these powers. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series I (Currency Derivatives) also covers the regulatory powers of SEBI under various Acts. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember that SEBI is the primary regulator for securities market oversight and has broad powers of inquiry.
